rulings.Case Title: PAMELA CARRARA VS. LOVINGUNIVERSITY.COM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Add Alter Ego Defendant To Judgment Pursuant To Ccp Section 187
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PAMELA CARRARA Plaintiff'S Motion To Add Alter Ego Defendant To Judgment Pursuant To Ccp Section 187 Plaintiff Pamela Carrara's motion to add alter ego defendant to judgment is granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed amended judgment submitted by Ms. Carrara. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PONIST LAW GROUP P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitration [Ccp Section 1281.6}; Mem Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2018, Line 4, PETITIONER PONIST LAW GROUP P.C., SEAN PONIST's Hearing On Petition For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitration [Ccp Section 1281.6}; Mem Of Points And Authorities Petitioners Ponist Law Group, P.C. and Sean E. Ponist's petition to appoint an arbitrator is continued to January 3, 2019, a day that counsel for respondents is apparently available. Unless the parties stipulate to an arbitrator before the hearing, the court intends to appoint one of the fifteen people listed in petitioner's initial memorandum as the arbitrator. On or before December 20, 2018 each side may send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org without copying the other side striking up to six of the fifteen persons. Respondents were properly served with the petition per CCP 1290.4 (a) because the parties' agreement provides that arbitration shall be conducted under the rules of the AAA and rule 43 of the AAA (Commercial Arbitration Rules) provides that service of process may be effected by personal or mail delivery to the party or its representatives. Therefore, service on respondents' representatives in the arbitration proceedings by personal delivery and overnight mail or email constitutes proper service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM F. WEBSTER JR. VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expneses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 29, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM WEBSTER's Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses Plaintiff William Webster Jr.'s motion for attorney's fees, costs and expenses is granted in large part. Mr. Webster is awarded fees of $26,040 and costs of $1,312.15 for a total of $27,352.50. Based on a review of the parties' papers and the court's own experience, the court determines that the hours and hourly rates claimed by counsel for Mr. Webster are within the reasonable range of the hours and rates for the services of plaintiffs' counsel and thus the court accepts Mr. Webster's proffered lodestar of $21,700. However, because both the hours and hourly rates are at the high end of a reasonable range, the court declines to award the requested 1.5 multiplier and instead awards a 1.2 multiplier due to contingent risk and excellent result. All costs claimed by Mr. Webster were reasonably incurred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Webster is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: DORA BARNES VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco Motion For Defendants Motion Requesting Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 29, 2018, Line 2, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco's Motion For Defendants Motion Requesting Sanctions. Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion of defendant City and County of San Francisco is granted. Plaintiff Dora Barnes has not fully complied with the court's previous order of September 18, 2018, which granted defendant's motion to compel plaintiff's response to defendant's form interrogatories and requests for production of documents. Plaintiff shall provide verification of her August 29, 2018 responses to defendant's form interrogatories by December 5, 2018. Plaintiff shall provide all previously withheld responsive documents by December 5, 2018. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions in the amount of $2,387 by December 10, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
